utterances.text: If you could advance to slide four, please.

chapters.summary: Jarboe describes the proposed seven-story, 53,000-square-foot building with 7,000 square feet of retail and 46,000 residential, totaling 54 units with 19 parking spaces. The unit mix emphasizes two- and three-bedroom units (65% of total) in response to community and council member concerns. 25% or 14 units would be permanently affordable under MIH option one.
chapters.title: Justin Jarboe on building details, unit mix, and parking provision

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: The development site is approximately 150 feet deep by 150 feet of frontage along Fresh Pond Road, just under 15,000 square feet in area.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: It was previously developed with two commercial buildings, both of which have been demolished.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: The proposed rezoning to the R6A district is generally considered a modest residential zoning district, which under the recently enacted City of Yes regulations has a permitted FAR of 3.9, of which 25 to 30%

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: would be required as affordable under the MIH program under options one or two.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: I'll note that a portion of the site being the last 25 feet along Bleeker Street, so the on the left hand side in the image,

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: would remain in the existing R5B zoning district and would be subject to the R5B regulations with reduced floor area and height allowances.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: The plans provided as part of the application show a seven-story building, again with 53,000 square feet in area, consisting of 7,000 square feet of retail

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: on the first floor and the remaining 46,000 square feet of residential floor area on floors two through seven, a total of 54 dwelling units with parking for 19 cars.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: If you can go to slide seven, please.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: With regard to the unit count, the plans reflect no studio units and a large number of two and three bedroom units, 35 of the 54 units, or 65% of the total unit mix.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: Providing larger units and providing parking where none is currently required represents the owner's response to concerns that were originally heard from the community, as well as from former Councilmember Holden and as well as current Councilmember Wong.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: In compliance with the MIH text change, it is anticipated that 25% or 14 units would be permanently affordable under option one of the program.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: If you can go to the next slide, please.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: In that regard, we note that Community Board 5 of Queens is currently anticipated to be on the list of the 12

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: community districts with the lowest creation of affordable housing, and thus applications such as this would be eligible for the Affordable Housing Fast Track application process, which commences in January of 2027.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: If you can go to slide 18, please.

chapters.summary: Jarboe details the building height of 73 feet with bulkhead, maximum 95 feet printed height, lower 50-foot height along Bleecker Street, and 19 voluntary parking spaces accessed from Bleecker Street. He notes the site is opposite a manufacturing district and LIRR tracks, near Stop & Store warehouse, Notre Dame Academy, and PS71, with comparable building sizes in the area. The site is a nine-minute walk to the M station.
chapters.title: Justin Jarboe on building height, parking access, and land use context

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: As permitted by the proposed R6A regulations, the building would rise to a height of 73 feet with a bulkhead setback and extending another 10 feet.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: The seventh floor would additionally be set back and the building overall would have a maximum printed height of 95 feet.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: The building would be substantially lower along the bleaker street, limited to 50 feet, with no construction at all along the lot line.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: 19 parking spaces would be provided at the first floor, which are provided voluntarily as parking is waived in the greater transit area.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: Parking would be accessed along Bleeker Street and no curb cuts would be provided along Fresh Pond Road.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: If you can go back to slide three.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: Great, thanks.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: While the existing housing stock along this block is smaller, two-story buildings, this block is opposite a manufacturing district and proximate to Long Island Railroad tracks,

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: and the proximity to manufacturing and a railroad usually make for a more appropriate location for multifamily development at a larger scale than the existing R5B permits.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: The site is opposite a mix of commercial manufacturing uses, including two automotive uses and a large stop-and-store facility across the street.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: To the east on the opposite side of Manahan Street, there are some newer four-story residential buildings, and the site itself is only one block from the intersection of Metropolitan Avenue and Fresh Pond Road, which are two heavily commercial thoroughfares with a CVS and a Pep Boys store is present.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: As you can see on the slide, there are existing buildings of comparable size in the area, including the stop and store warehouse I mentioned before, the Notre Dame Academy, and PS71, which are further up to the west with the heights indicated on the map.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: The site is a nine-minute walk to the M station and has access to multiple buses that serve the area and is located in the newly defined greater transit zone.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: Can you go to slide five, please?

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: There we go.

chapters.summary: Jarboe explains the rezoning area includes four existing buildings not owned by the applicant that are not anticipated as redevelopment sites. Community Board 5 approved unanimously with a condition that the northerly portion be amended from R6A to R6B. The Borough President also gave a positive recommendation.
chapters.title: Justin Jarboe on rezoning area, non-applicant properties, and CB5 approval

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: The rezoning area extending from Bleeker Street to Menahan Street will include four existing buildings that are not owned or controlled by the applicant and are not anticipated as redevelopment sites as they appear to be occupied and are under individual

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: ownership and would likely need to be merged and vacated in order to create a new development site.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: Next slide.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: The application received unanimous approval from Community Board 5 with the condition that the northerly part of the rezoning area north of Bleecker Street be amended from R6A in the proposal to R6B,

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: involving a property that is not owned or controlled by the applicant, along with a positive recommendation from the borough president.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: That concludes our presentation.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: We are available to answer any questions you might have.

chapters.summary: Chair Louis asks about providing more parking than required and what guarantees can ensure the additional parking will be built. Jarboe confirms 19 spaces are proposed where zero are required under City of Yes transit zone rules, and the community desperately wants off-street parking. He suggests memorializing the parking commitment could be discussed further.
chapters.title: Chair Louis questions parking provision beyond zoning requirements

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Thank you so much for your testimony.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: I have a few questions about this proposal.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Can you confirm that your proposal has more parking spaces than is required by zoning?

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: And if so, what guarantees can you provide to ensure you will provide additional parking, which is needed in this neighborhood?

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: So at this time, we propose 19 parking spaces.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: Under the recently enacted City of Yes regulations, this area is in a newly defined transit zone, so no parking is required based on the unit mix that we're proposing.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: So that's 19 more than would be required.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: I know it's in the builder's interest to provide parking in this neighborhood.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: That's one of the main issues we heard throughout this process, that the community desperately wants to see off-street parking.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: In terms of ensuring that those parking spaces could be there, that's something we could discuss further about memorializing that in some way.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Chair Louis asks about plans for ground-level commercial and community facility spaces. Jarboe explains the applicant owns a pizzeria across the street and intends to place a similar use or existing tenant in the commercial space. No community facility is currently proposed, though potential exists for the future.
chapters.title: Chair Louis questions commercial and community facility space plans

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: What are your plans for the community facility?

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: and ground level commercial spaces and how will you go about identifying occupants for those spaces?

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: So the applicant is an owner of a pizzeria and is in the eating and drinking business and the pizzeria is actually located across the street so I believe the intention is to put a similar use in the space or one of the existing tenants that he currently has in his portfolio.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: In terms of community facility space at this time, none is proposed, although there is potential for that in the future.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Okay.

chapters.summary: Chair Louis asks for the proposed unit breakdown including MIH option and affordability levels. Jarboe reports 19 one-bedroom, 27 two-bedroom, and 8 three-bedroom units totaling 54, with 14 units permanently affordable under MIH option one at 25%. He offers to send detailed information.
chapters.title: Chair Louis questions unit breakdown and MIH affordability option

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Can you share your proposed unit breakdown, including the selected MIH option affordability levels and the mix of unit sizes?

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: So if you go to slide...

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: So we actually have them in two separate slides, but perhaps if you can go to Z1 on the architectural sheets.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: As I mentioned before, it's predominantly two and three bedrooms.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: That would be 65%.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: The presentation number is called Z1?

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: It's a sheet Z1.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: I don't know what page it is.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: My apologies.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Page 11.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: Page 11.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Is it possible to pull up a presentation?

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: If you want me to share just one minute.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: We should be able to pull it up.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Okay, well, while we are pulling it up, maybe you can just walk us through.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: Okay, so there would be 19 one-bedroom units, 27 two-bedroom units, and eight three-bedroom units for a total of 54 units.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: And we can send you this information.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: No problem.

chapters.summary: Chair Louis asks whether the applicant engaged property owners within the rezoning area who are not part of the application. Adam Rothkrug confirms they did not engage other owners because those properties are developed with inhabited buildings on smaller 25x100 sites and are not anticipated as redevelopment sites.
chapters.title: Chair Louis questions engagement with non-applicant property owners in rezoning area

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Have you engaged property owners within the rezoning area who are not part of the application?

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: Perhaps Adam is on the line for.

utterances.speaker: Justin Jarboe
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: Adam Rothkrug
utterances.text: Yes, we did not engage other owners in the area because as noted, it doesn't appear that those are really development sites.

utterances.speaker: Adam Rothkrug
utterances.text: They're developed with existing inhabited buildings.

utterances.speaker: Adam Rothkrug
utterances.text: They're mostly smaller sites, 25 by 100 sites.

utterances.speaker: Adam Rothkrug
utterances.text: Again, it's not anticipated that those would be developed in the foreseeable future.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Chair Louis excuses the panel with no colleague questions. No members of the public signed up to testify. The hearing on LUs 104–105 regarding 63-02 Fresh Pond Road is closed and items are laid over.
chapters.title: Panel excused; no public testimony; Hearing 2 closed and items laid over

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: This panel is now, sorry, I will now invite my colleagues to ask questions.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Does anybody have questions for this panel?

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Seeing none, this panel is now excused.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Thank you so much for making time to be here today.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Chair, nurse, and the council.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Council, are there any members of the public who wish to testify remotely or in person regarding this proposal?

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: No, not at this time.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: There's no one signed up in person or online.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: For members of the public here to testify, please note that we're...

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Stand at ease.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: If there's no one...

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Hold on one second.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: If there are any members of the public who wish to testify regarding this proposal remotely, please press the raise hand button now.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: If you are in person, please identify yourself to one of the sergeant at arms.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: This is the last call.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Being there are no members of the public who wish to testify regarding LUs 104 and 105 relating to the 6302 Fresh Pond Road mixed-use residential rezoning proposal, the public hearing is now closed and items are now laid over.

chapters.summary: Chair Louis opens the hearing on LUs 106–107 regarding 1160 Pugsley Avenue in the Union Port neighborhood of the Bronx, CM Farías's district. The proposal involves a zoning map and MIH text amendment for a mixed-use affordable housing project with approximately 104 apartments and a grocery store through the Fresh Food Store program, up to 31 permanently affordable.
chapters.title: Hearing 3 opening: 1160 Pugsley Avenue rezoning in Union Port, the Bronx

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: I will now open the first public hearing on LUs 106 and 107 regarding the 1160 Pugsley Avenue, a mixed-use residential rezoning proposal in the Union Port neighborhood of the Bronx and Councilmember Fadies'district.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: The proposal involves a zoning map and an MIH zoning tax amendment applicant is seeking to build a mixed-use affordable housing project with approximately 104 apartments and a grocery store through the Fresh Food Store program.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Mandatory inclusionary housing.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: would be mapped as part of this application, which would require up to 31 of the apartments to be permanently affordable, depending on the affordability option selected.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: For anyone wishing to testify regarding this proposal remotely, if you have not already done so, you must register online by visiting the council's website at council.nyc.gov backslash land use.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: For anyone with us in person, please see one of the sergeant at arms to submit a speaker card.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: If you prefer to submit written testimony, you can always do so by emailing it to landyousetestimony at council.nyc.gov.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: I now recognize Councilmember Fadias for remarks.

chapters.summary: Councilmember Amanda Farías provides opening remarks, noting the East Bronx urgently needs housing that is affordable, livable, and connected to community needs. She highlights the 104 income-restricted units with multi-bedroom apartments and the 10,900-square-foot fresh grocery store addressing food deserts. She notes CB9 and Borough President Gibson recommended approval and emphasizes commitments to local hiring, MWBE participation, and building security.
chapters.title: CM Farías opening remarks on 1160 Pugsley Avenue and food desert needs

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Thank you so much, Chair Lewis.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Good morning, everyone, and to the members of the Subcommittee on Zoning and Franchises.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Thank you both to the subcommittee, the staff, and the applicant team for the opportunity to speak today regarding the application put in for the 1160 Pugsley Avenue rezoning.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: My office has followed this project closely throughout the public review process to ensure it delivers meaningful, practical benefits for the East Bronx.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: In our communities, conversations about development cannot focus solely on the builds.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: They must also be about whether a project thoughtfully responds to the people, the families, and the neighborhoods that are already there.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: The East Bronx urgently needs more housing, but we must ensure that it is housing that is affordable, livable, and deeply connected to the everyday needs of our residents.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: From our comprehensive review, this application addresses those core neighborhood priorities.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: The proposal would change the current designation to an R7A-C-24 district to bring a

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: 104 income-restricted rental units to the site.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Crucially, this includes a healthy mix of unit sizes, including 32 two-bedrooms and eight three-bedroom multi-bedroom apartments, which are vital for supporting local families, especially those that are growing.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Alongside the residential footprint,

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: This project delivers something our neighborhood has been starved for for years, a massive 10,900 square foot fresh grocery store right on the ground floor.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: It is a blueprint for a healthier, stronger neighborhood.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: We are talking about bringing housing security, local retail convenience, and healthy food access together under one roof.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: After taking an extensive look at this application, especially as the representative of an East Bronx district where food deserts remain a serious issue, I'm encouraged by the inclusion of a proposed fresh grocery store.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: I also want to note that both the Bronx Community Board 9 and Borough President Vanessa Gibson recommended approval of this application.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: I'm glad we're able to find alignment around the expectation that developments in our neighborhoods must include firm commitments to local hiring, strong MWBE participation, building security, and accessible resident amenities to help address the affordability crisis facing New Yorkers today.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: These, as well as the planned outdoor rooftop recreation center, would be great contributing factors to the long-term viability of the grocery provider.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: I look forward to reviewing the final application documents and seeing these commitments clearly reflected.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Our priority is making sure new developments in our neighborhoods are thoughtful, accountable, and prioritizing the needs of the people who live here.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: The housing crisis.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: is urgent, therefore we must be critical when asking our questions and demanding higher standards to the buffer to buffer the negative impacts housing and food insecurity is having on our neighbors and our communities.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: I look forward to continuing to hear from the applicant team today and to ensuring these critical commitments are firmly reflected as this application advances to the council's review.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Thank you, Chair Lewis, for the time.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Thank you, Council Member.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: I will now call the applicant panel for this proposal, which consists of Lisa Orantia.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Okay.

chapters.summary: Chair Louis calls Lisa Orantia from Ackerman LLP to present. Council staff administers the oath.
chapters.title: Applicant panel sworn in for 1160 Pugsley Avenue

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Good morning.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Could you please raise your right hand and state your name for the record?

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Lisa Orantia from Ackerman LLP.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Do you swear to tell the truth in nothing but the truth in your testimony this morning in response to council member questions?

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Yes.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: You may begin your testimony, which is limited to 10 minutes, and you should start to conclude your presentation at 8 minutes.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: When you begin your presentation, please restate your name and the name of your organization.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: You may begin.

chapters.summary: Lisa Orantia presents on behalf of 1160-1178 Pugsley Avenue LLC, whose principal Jesus Diaz has 40 years in the supermarket business and 16 years of site ownership. The application includes rezoning from R5 to R7A with C2-4 overlay, MIH mapping for options one and two, and a non-ULERP fresh food store certification. The project area is on Pugsley Avenue between Gleason and Powell Avenues, a six-minute walk from Grant Circle transit hub.
chapters.title: Lisa Orantia presents 1160 Pugsley Avenue project overview and rezoning actions

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Good afternoon, Chair Lewis, members of the subcommittee, and thank you, Councilmember Farias, for your comments.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: My name is Lisa Orantia from Ackerman LLP, and I'm here on behalf of the applicant, 1160-1178 Pugsley Avenue, LLC.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The principal of the applicant company, Jesus Diaz, has been in the supermarket business for 40 years and has owned the development site for 16 years.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: This application has three actions, rezoning,

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: from R5 to R7A with a C24 overlay, a mapping of an MIH area for options one and two, and a non-ULERP action for a fresh food store certification.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Next slide, please.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The project area is located on Pugsley Avenue between Gleason and Powell Avenues, and both non-conforming and conforming commercial uses exist nearby.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Five and six story buildings face the project area and notably the grant circle transportation hub is a six minute walk from the project site.

chapters.summary: Orantia explains the existing R5 district allows only five stories and 2 FAR, discouraging affordable housing investment. The proposed R7A/C2-4 district would allow an 11-story building with 5.01 FAR with affordable housing, appropriate on an 80-foot-wide street with transit access, consistent with surrounding five- and six-story buildings. The site currently has small stores and open parking.
chapters.title: Lisa Orantia on current R5 zoning limitations and proposed R7A district

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Next slide please.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Pugsley Avenue is on an 80 foot wide street.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The low density R5 district allows up to five stories and two FAR, which doesn't encourage the production of affordable housing or investment in the existing non-conforming commercial uses.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: There are no residential uses on the site, which is currently improved with small stores and an open parking area that would be replaced by the new building.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Next slide, please.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The new R7AC24 proposed district would allow a maximum of 11-story

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: building with 5.01 FAR with the provision of affordable housing instead of the current 4-5 story and 1.5 or 2 FAR.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The proposed increase in density and building height are appropriate on a wide street.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: access to public transportation.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: It would allow a new building envelope that's consistent with what exists in the surrounding area.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: And it would be accompanied by required affordable housing to help alleviate the housing crisis and would allow local retail uses to continue as of right.

chapters.summary: Orantia describes the eight-story, 92,000-square-foot residential building with 104 affordable units over 13,000 square feet of commercial space including a fresh food store, 2,700 square feet of outdoor recreation, and 32 parking spaces. Energy-efficient features include green roof areas, energy-star appliances, low-flow plumbing, and renewable construction materials.
chapters.title: Lisa Orantia on proposed building: 104 affordable units, fresh food store, energy features

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Next slide, please.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The proposed building is eight stories tall.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: It would utilize the additional floor area in the residential portion generated by the fresh food store bonus.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: It would have a total of about 92,000 square feet of residential use with 104.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Affordable dwelling units.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: This is a hundred percent affordable residential building over 13,000 square feet of commercial floor area, which would be divided between the fresh food store plus a smaller retail establishment

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: and approximately

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: 2,700 square feet of outdoor recreation space.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Also included are 32 permitted residential parking spaces.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: And the exterior design is brick and architectural panel finished to reflect the buildings in the area.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Energy efficient features would include a roof with green areas, energy and sound efficient windows, energy star appliances, low flow plumbing fixtures, and construction materials derived from natural and renewable sources.

chapters.summary: Orantia details HPD financing under the mixed-income option of the new construction program targeting 60% AMI average. The unit mix includes 19 studios, 45 one-bedrooms, 33 two-bedrooms, 6 three-bedrooms, and one two-bedroom super unit, with rents at 40, 50, 60, 80, and 100% AMI levels. Two- and three-bedroom units comprise 38% of the mix.
chapters.title: Lisa Orantia on HPD financing, unit mix, and AMI levels

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Next slide, please.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The development would utilize HPD financing for 100% affordable multifamily rental project under the mixed income option of the new construction finance program, which has a target

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: average of 60% AMI.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The unit sizes and breakdown by type are shown here with income and rents at proposed 40, 50, 60, 80, and 100% AMI levels.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The design is for 19 studios, 45 one bedrooms, 33 two bedrooms, six three bedrooms, and includes one two bedroom for a live-in super.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Next slide, please.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The bedroom mix and rent by AMI level are shown here.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: We're showing 38% two and three bedroom units.

chapters.summary: Orantia presents the site plan showing the building stepping down to five stories adjoining R5 neighbors, an eight-foot side yard, 1,600 square feet of landscaped area, and a green roof. Residential parking for 32 cars is in the cellar with access on Gleason Avenue. Commercial entries face Pugsley Avenue with loading on Powell Avenue. CB9 voted in favor and the Borough President recommended approval.
chapters.title: Lisa Orantia on site plan, parking, and community board approval

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Next slide, please.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The site plan shows how the building will step down to five stories adjoining the two-story buildings in the neighboring R5 district.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: There's an eight-foot-wide side yard that separates the new building from adjoining buildings, and there's a green roof over the first floor.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: This is going to be about 1,600 square feet of landscaped area.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Next slide, please.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Residential parking, this slide shows the residential parking for 32 cars in the cellar.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The residential lobby on Gleason Avenue with access to the parking garage also on Gleason.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Commercial entries will be on Pugsley Avenue and the fresh loading berth would be on Powell Avenue.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The next slide, please.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Showing you the upper floor plans.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Next slide.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: This shows the top residential floor with a roof area of 2,700 square feet for landscape recreation.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Next slide, please.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: We had Community Board 9 voting in favor of the application.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The applicant was responsive to the community's requests.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: And we also have Borough President recommending approval.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: And that concludes the presentation.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Thank you very much.

chapters.summary: Chair Louis asks about HPD conversations given the agency's extensive pipeline and potential years-long wait for small project funding. Orantia reports the applicant is working with Urban Impact Capital Partners, submitted a project intake sheet with HPD earlier this year, and is committed to providing 100% affordable housing, willing to wait and follow the process.
chapters.title: Chair Louis questions HPD financing status and interim property plans

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Thank you so much for your presentation.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: I have one quick question.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: I'm going to hand it over to Councilmember Farias.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Given that this proposed as an affordable housing project, what is the status of your conversations with HPD?

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Given the extent of HPD's pipeline and that it can take years to obtain funding from HPD for a small project like this, what do you plan on doing with the property in the meantime?

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: So the applicant is working with an affordable housing consultant.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The name is Urban Impact Capital Partners, and they have submitted a new project intake sheet with HBD at the beginning of this year.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: And this applicant is committed to providing 100% affordable housing at this location.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: And, you know, we're willing to wait and follow the process and continue to engage with HBD.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: I'm going to hand it over to Council Member Fadi to ask for questions.

chapters.summary: CM Farías asks about community board concerns regarding existing supermarkets in the area and the competitive nature of the developer's other Bronx locations. Orantia explains the applicant has 40 years of supermarket experience, the fresh food program provides healthy food options within walking distance for residents, and the store will operate as a Key Food with periodic compliance reports and a restrictive declaration.
chapters.title: CM Farías questions supermarket location and food desert rationale

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Thank you, Chair, and thank you for the presentation.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: We're super familiar already.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: We've had so many conversations before we got to this point, so I'm glad we're here.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Happy to hear that commitment on seeing it all the way through with the HPD term sheet.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: My, I guess just like for the record for me, what I'd like to kind of put out there is some of the conversation that happened in community board was around how there's several

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: supermarkets or a couple, not several, a couple of supermarkets in the area and that they don't, some folks didn't necessarily agree with this being a location.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: You folks already know my opinion on that with the food desert that we're in.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: I believe this is a suitable location based off of the square footage.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: I mean that the radius in the area of accessibility.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: But can you talk a little bit more about that and talk about the other locations where the developer already has his supermarkets and the competitive nature of it or not in terms of serving.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Bronx sites.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Sure.

utterances.text: So as I mentioned at the start, this applicant is an experienced supermarket owner.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: He's been in the business for 40 years, has other locations in the Bronx and outside the city,

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: is very familiar with how to run a successful supermarket business, and is eager to bring the fresh food program to this location, which,

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: as you know, provides for a variety of healthy food options.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: And it's designed for residents to be able to walk to the store.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: So it's ideal for people who live within the surrounding area.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: They don't have to use a car to access healthy food options.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: The fresh food program requires periodic compliance reports, restrictive declaration, and the applicant is fully prepared to follow through with that process.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: They'll be engaged directly in the operation of the supermarket.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: It's going to be run as key food.

chapters.summary: CM Farías asks about plans for the additional commercial space adjacent to the supermarket. Orantia says the applicant is open to community suggestions for what would best serve the neighborhood. Farías expresses interest in continuing the conversation through the application process.
chapters.title: CM Farías questions additional commercial space tenant plans

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: And do you have any idea of the additional commercial space that will be adjacent to the market of what that makeup is going to look like or any ideas towards what is looking to be placed there?

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Right now, the applicant is open.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: Whatever is most suitable for the community, we're happy to receive any.

utterances.speaker: Lisa Orantia
utterances.text: kind of suggestions or recommendations of what would best serve the community.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Okay, great.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Looking forward to continue the conversation as we move through the application process.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Thank you, Chair.

chapters.summary: Chair Louis excuses the panel with no colleague questions. No members of the public wish to testify. The hearing on LUs 106–107 regarding 1160 Pugsley Avenue is closed and items are laid over.
chapters.title: Panel excused; no public testimony; Hearing 3 closed and items laid over

utterances.text: I now invite my colleagues to ask questions.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Anyone has questions for the panel?

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Seeing none.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: If there are any, oh, sorry, I want to excuse the panel.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Thank you so much for your testimony today.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: If there are any members of the public who wish to testify regarding this proposal remotely, please press the raise hand button now, or if you are in person, please identify yourself to one of the sergeant at arms.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Being there, no members of the public who wish to testify regarding LUs 106 and 107 relating to 1160 Pugsley Avenue mixed-use residential rezoning proposal, the public hearing is now closed and items are now laid over.

chapters.summary: Chair Louis opens the hearing on LUs 108–109 regarding 1166 Bedford Avenue in Bedford-Stuyvesant, Brooklyn, CM Osei's district. The proposal involves a zoning map and MIH text amendment for a mixed-use affordable housing project with approximately 144 apartments, up to 43 permanently affordable.
chapters.title: Hearing 4 opening: 1166 Bedford Avenue rezoning in Bed-Stuy, Brooklyn

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: I will now open the public hearing on LUs 108 and 109 regarding 1166 Bedford Avenue, a mixed-use residential rezoning proposal in Bed-Stuy, Brooklyn, in Councilmember Osei's district.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: The proposal involves a zoning map and an MIH zoning text amendment.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Applicant is seeking to build a mixed-use affordable housing project with approximately 144 apartments.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Mandatory inclusionary housing would be mapped as part of this application, which would require that up to 43 of the apartments be permanently affordable, depending on the affordability option selected.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: For anyone wishing to testify regarding this proposal remotely, if you have not already done so, you must register online by visiting the council's website at council.nyc.gov backslash land use.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: For anyone with us in person, please see one of the sergeant at arms to submit a speaker card.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: If you would prefer to submit written testimony, you can always do so by emailing it to landusest testimony at council.nyc.gov.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: I will now open the applicant panel for this proposal, which consists of Andrew Albrecht, Pierre Downing, Imam Rodney Rashid, and Shaniqua Henry.

chapters.summary: Chair Louis calls the applicant panel—Andrew Albrecht, Pierre Downing, Imam Rodney Rashid, and Shaniqua Henry. Council staff administers the oath to all four panelists.
chapters.title: Applicant panel sworn in for 1166 Bedford Avenue

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: For those four individuals who are just called, could you please raise your right hand?

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Rodney Rashid.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Pierre Downing.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Do you swear to tell the truth and nothing but the truth in your testimony this morning and in response to council member questions?

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Yes.

utterances.text: Yes.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
